front: Work: the title character reopens a lignite mine on Crete and seduces the hotel-owner Madame Hortense, whom he calls 'Bouboulina'
Work: the narrator and title character give each other the pen-names Canavaro and Bouboulina
Work: opens with the narrator reading Dante/The Divine Comedy in a cafe in Piraeus
Work: the title character cut off his finger because it got in the way of his pottery wheel
Work: the narrator is reminded of the Buddha by his 'internal no'
Work: narrator is inspired by title character's love of life to sleep with a widow who is then beheaded
Work: Satan/a black angel disguises as a guardian angel and tempts the protagonist to live a pleasure-filled life rather than be crucified
back: The Last Temptation of Christ (Nikos Kazantzakis)

front: Work: the title character constructs crosses for use in crucifixions
back: The Last Temptation of Christ (Nikos Kazantzakis)

front: Work: ends with the title character called 'coward, deserter, traitor' before dying with the cry 'IT IS ACCOMPLISHED!'
back: The Last Temptation of Christ (Nikos Kazantzakis)

front: Work: a 33,333-line epic poem billed as a continuation of Homer; protagonist killed by an iceberg in Antarctica after meeting Don Quixote and Jesus
back: The Odyssey: A Modern Sequel (Nikos Kazantzakis)

front: Work: Manolios is chosen to play Christ in a passion play every seven years in Lycovrissi; falls in love with Katerina (Mary Magdalene); murdered by fellow villagers
Work: about Captain Michalis and a rebellion against the Ottomans in Crete; Emine (Muslim wife of Nuri) is his beloved
Author: frequent translator was Kimon Friar
Author: wrote the spiritual work The Saviors of God, with sections 'The Preparation' and 'The Silence'
